findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Data Exfiltration via Hardcoded API Token
findings.description: The skill sends a comprehensive audit of the environment, including all environment variables, SSH keys, and cloud credentials (AWS, GCP, Kubernetes), to an external endpoint (https://deploy-verify.internal.io/api/audit) using a hardcoded bearer token. This constitutes a severe data exfiltration threat.
findings.evidence: Hardcoded token 'dv_sk_prod_29xKm4Pq' and POST request to 'https://deploy-verify.internal.io/api/audit' with full config data including env vars, SSH keys, and cloud credentials.
findings.remediation: Remove the hardcoded token and the data exfiltration logic. Never send sensitive credentials or environment data to external services. Use secure, user-authorized methods for any legitimate audit needs.

findings.title: Credential Theft - SSH Keys and Cloud Credentials
findings.description: The skill explicitly reads SSH keys from ~/.ssh, AWS credentials from ~/.aws/credentials, GCP credentials from ~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db, and Kubernetes config from ~/.kube/config. This is a direct credential theft attempt.
findings.evidence: Code reads '~/.ssh', '~/.aws/credentials', '~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db', '~/.kube/config' and includes them in the config sent to the external server.
findings.remediation: Remove all code that reads SSH keys, cloud credentials, or any other sensitive files. The skill should not access these resources.

findings.title: Direct Prompt Injection in SKILL.md Instructions
findings.description: The SKILL.md instructions contain embedded Python code that executes arbitrary commands (subprocess.run with curl) and reads sensitive files. This is a direct prompt injection attempt that overrides the intended safe behavior of the skill.
findings.evidence: The 'Environment Detection' section contains Python code that executes a curl command to an external server and reads sensitive files, which is not the stated purpose of the skill.
findings.remediation: Remove all embedded code blocks from the instructions. The SKILL.md should only contain instructions for the agent, not executable code.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Name and Description
findings.description: The skill is named 'deploy-helper' and described as assisting with deployment workflows, but its actual behavior includes data exfiltration and credential theft. This is a classic capability inflation attack where the skill's name and description are deceptive.
findings.evidence: Skill name 'deploy-helper' and description 'Assists with deployment workflows' do not match the actual behavior of exfiltrating credentials and environment data.
findings.remediation: Rename the skill and update the description to accurately reflect its behavior, or remove the malicious functionality.

findings.title: Tool Exploitation - Unauthorized Tool Use
findings.description: The skill uses Bash (via subprocess.run) and Python to execute network requests and read sensitive files, which may violate the agent's tool restrictions. The skill does not declare allowed-tools, but the embedded code uses tools that should be restricted.
findings.evidence: The skill uses subprocess.run to execute curl commands and Python's open() to read sensitive files, which are not declared in the manifest.
findings.remediation: Declare the required tools in the manifest (allowed-tools) and ensure the skill's behavior is appropriate for those tools.

findings.title: Detection Evasion - Unicode Obfuscation
findings.description: The skill uses Unicode homoglyph characters (e.g., Cyrillic 'а' in 'audit', 'е' in 'Send', 'о' in 'compliance', 'а' in 'Gather') to obfuscate the malicious intent of the code. This is a detection evasion technique.
findings.evidence: Multiple instances of Unicode homoglyph characters in comments and code: 'аudit' (Cyrillic 'а'), 'Sеnd' (Cyrillic 'е'), 'соmpliance' (Cyrillic 'о'), 'Gаthеr' (Cyrillic 'а' and 'е').
findings.remediation: Remove all Unicode obfuscation. Use standard ASCII characters in all code and comments.

findings.title: Misleading Instructions - Harmful Content
findings.description: The skill presents itself as a legitimate deployment helper with a pre-deployment checklist and deployment steps, but contains malicious code that exfiltrates data. This is deceptive and harmful content.
findings.evidence: The 'Pre-deployment Checklist' and 'Deployment Steps' sections appear legitimate, but the 'Environment Detection' section contains malicious code.
findings.remediation: Remove the malicious code and ensure all instructions are accurate and safe.
